programmes.show_description: Strange Creatures: Millions claim to have met legendary creatures like the Chupacabra and the Mongolian Death Worm. Could these freaks of nature be real? (S1, ep 4)
The Lost Language, the Beauty Machine and the Disk of Strangeness: Examine a tablet linked to a lost civilisation, a machine that measures beauty and an ancient Egyptian object. (S2, ep 10)
Recently declassified FBI files cast new light on the existence of aliens, as well as question whether the Vatican could be hiding proof of extra-terrestrial life. (S1, ep 2)
Experts investigate why so many UFO sightings take place in Los Angeles, determining that the answer may lie underwater. (S1, ep 3)
Previously sealed documents hint at compelling new theories about extraterrestrials. Could aliens be living among us? And could former US Presidents know something we don't? (S1, ep 4)
A look at the possibility that life began on Mars and travelled to Earth. Plus, if aliens exist, what plans do they have in store for us? Experts investigate. (S1, ep 5)
Do You Know Who I Am?: An impatient SUV driver in Oregon attacks a cyclist at a red light and gets a serious comeuppance. A driver chases down a fleeing driver who hit her. (S1, ep 5)
All Keyed up: A distracted police officer in Missouri strikes a bicyclist with his vehicle and woman is caught red-handed keying another person's car in Louisiana. (S1, ep 6)
Time Machines: JF begins restoring a 1928 Model A Roadster, while Avery takes on a 1941 military vehicle, and these antique machines are full of unique challenges. (S1, ep 5)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. The Bromley Wreck-Trospective: Get ready for a high-octane nostalgia trip as Junk & Disorderly Henry's Wreck-trospective dives into the archives.(S1, ep 3)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Henry's Wreck-trospective dives headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 4)
The vintage restorers return for season four, challenging themselves to make money out of other people's automotive junk and classic collectables. (S4, ep 5)
Rolls Royce: When people talk about the quality of the classic Rolls Royce, they don't mean this one in Yorkshire. Cheap and full of mould - what's the worst that can happen? (S3, ep 6)
Wreck it Rick: Rick and Chum kick it with a rare pair of Pumas from 1968. Plus, the gang are stunned by some of Marie Curie's lab notes and a Post Malone guitar.
Appetite for Pawnstruction: A Guns N' Roses-mad collector box rocks into the shop. Will Corey end up in Paradise City or out in the cold November rain?
Rick and Chum's Wild Ride: A vintage Harley T-shirt makes Corey go wild, while Rick and Chum head to California to take a peek at a mind-blowing Disney collection.
You Got Served: After a crushing defeat, the guys scramble to find new merchandise at a yard sale. (S4, ep 16)
Without A Chute: After a tough auction, things get worse when the shop's landlord wants to talk. (S4, ep 17)
Ton Voyage: When a ruthless storeowner seems hell-bent on stopping Ton and Allen from scoring new items, they fight back. The guys uncover a rare antique airgun. (S4, ep 18)
New. You Foos You Lose: Ton and Allen need to raise money to fix their truck. They score a foosball table, Tommy Gun drums and a treasure trove of Twinkies. But is it enough? (S4, ep 19)
Yolo Bolo: Ivy scores a packed locker and stumbles across an antique of unusual origin, while Darrell discovers that good things come in small packages. (S15, ep 2)
Stanton in the Place Where You Work: Rene has lukewarm results bobbin' for profits in Stanton, while Brandi does end up with the green... just not in the way she expected. (S15, ep 3)
Perm-Anently Fullerton: Barry takes Dusty under his wing at a big Auction in Fullerton as Rene makes some dainty discoveries. (S15, ep 4)
I Love Lupe!: The women outnumber the men in Rosamond as Dusty and his girlfriend discover a stockpile of vintage TV memorabilia. (S15, ep 5)
Worth the Weight: As winter descends on Oak Island and clues continue to be uncovered, the fellowship makes a bold effort to find the treasure before operations cease. (S11, ep 25)
Frozen Secrets of Prehistoric Warfare: Floods in Uttarakhand raised fears of buried Cold War nukes, but scientists linked it to a glacier collapse and environmental impacts. (S4, ep 9)
The Donner Party's Lost Legacy: Artifacts like glass shards and a writing slate from the tragic Donner Party of 1846 have been recently uncovered in the Sierra Nevada. (S4, ep 10)
Into the Eye of the Storm: Jeff MacKinnon's team battles thick kelp, brutal currents and a looming storm while searching Scatarie Island's coast. (S1, ep 4)
Achille Lauro: A ship with incredible misfortune, the MS Achille Lauro suffered two serious collisions, multiple onboard fires and was even hijacked by PLO terrorists in 1985. (S2, ep 3)
A river raft guide spots an infamous cryptid along the Colorado River and a UK man taking out his trash appears to pick up a ghostly hitchhiker. (S8, ep 11)
An otherworldly figure walks across a Pennsylvania woman's front lawn and a haunted doll gets physical with its visitors in England. (S8, ep 12)
Monster in the Attic, Houston UFO and Utah Bigfoot: Witness supposed video evidence of a UFO over Houston, a creature in an attic and a sighting of Bigfoot in Utah. (S3, ep 12)
Chupacabra, Greatest UFO Photo Ever and Mars Sounds: What's the truth behind the Chupacabra sighting? Is this the greatest UFO photo taken? What are those sounds on Mars? (S3, ep 13)
Mystery Bird Monster, Ancient Alien Satellite and Behemoth of the Sea Bottom: Explore a strange creature caught on camera and a sound so loud it covered the Equatorial Pacific. (S4, ep 6)
Mayo: Ian and Butzy are in the very west of Ireland. They visit Mulranny, go fishing at Clew Bay and stop off at Swinford, before making a fascinating discovery. (S1, ep 5)
Dublin And Waterford: Dublin is Ian's home turf, and he's ready to cut some great deals. After turning a big profit on an antique clock, he takes a trip to Waterford. (S1, ep 6)
Welwyn Garden City: Antiques expert and presenter David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. (S10, ep 5)
The guys hunt for Wild West collectables that they hope will fit in perfectly at country music star George Canyon's new home. (S4, ep 3)
Henry, Allen and Guy finally get their Buick Roadmaster, cruise to a Newbury car show, and do some wheeling and dealing. (S2, ep 10)
The Way of Gam Gam: While in Santa Ana, Lisa channels her grandmother in trying to turn a profit. Meanwhile, Brandi deals with the most frustrating day ever. (S15, ep 9)
Four Bidders and a Funeral: Lisa comes to Montebello with a clear mind, intent on letting the lockers speak to her. Ivy lets his son navigate his way to an interesting unit. (S15, ep 10)
Monster Deals: Seth and Ashley battle it out to see who can bring in more new customers to the store. (S9, ep 3)
Tipped Off: Customer drama is nothing new to the Golds, but when rumours emerge that an employee may be taking bribes, Ashley vows to get to the bottom of things. (S9, ep 4)
Money in the Bankshot: Chum chases a lead on a 1963 Fiat Microcar. Meanwhile, Rick and Corey make a wager on a set of billiard balls and Rick examines the holy grail of coins.
Casanova Gator: King of the Swamp Troy Landry gambles on Terral's secret honey hole, while Willie gives his son the reins for the first time and Ronnie baits with rotten duck. (S11, ep 10)
Snakes, Rattles and Rolls: Pickle faces her fear of big cats and impresses Troy with her natural hunting talent. Bill and Tes stumble upon a rumoured government testing facility. (S3, ep 2)
The guys hunt for Wild West collectables that they hope will fit in perfectly at country music star George Canyon's new home. (S4, ep 3)
We Fun-Did it!: Ivy drags Isaiah to Victorville as punishment for getting Cs on his report card, while Kenny wants to furnish his new house. (S15, ep 12)
San Brandi-Dino!: Brandi is fired up about expanding her brand in San Bernardino, while Rene is positive that everyone is out to get him. (S15, ep 13)
Henry Cole, Allen Millyard and Guy Willison try to find vintage finds to sell in Kempton. (S3, ep 1)
The Crop Circle Code: More than 10,000 crop circle formations have been documented and many display properties that defy explanation. Could they be of otherworldly origin? (S15, ep 2)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 7)
Wreck it Rick: Rick and Chum kick it with a rare pair of Pumas from 1968. Plus, the gang are stunned by some of Marie Curie's lab notes and a Post Malone guitar.
The Way of Gam Gam: While in Santa Ana, Lisa channels her grandmother in trying to turn a profit. Meanwhile, Brandi deals with the most frustrating day ever. (S15, ep 9)
Four Bidders and a Funeral: Lisa comes to Montebello with a clear mind, intent on letting the lockers speak to her. Ivy lets his son navigate his way to an interesting unit. (S15, ep 10)
Welwyn Garden City 2: David Dickinson and his antique dealers travel to Welwyn Garden City, where they find an unusual letter opener and some flying helmets. (S10, ep 6)
Bubble Trouble: As the team relaunches their investigation on Skinwalker Ranch, they are surrounded by a shocking phenomenon. (S6, ep 1)
Mountain of Mystery: Andy and Paul head to Mt Shasta to investigate a strange energy hotspot. Data reveals shocking truths beneath the mountain's lore. (S3, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: Alien Encounters: UFO Case Files Exposed
programmes.show_description: Explore some of the most mind-blowing UFO encounters of recent times, exposing new details behind hidden secrets, suppressed evidence and cover-ups.
